metadata.name: FACT_TRANSACTIONS
metadata.comment: Fact table containing transaction details from the Stellar network.

LEDGER_SEQUENCE.name: LEDGER_SEQUENCE
LEDGER_SEQUENCE.comment: The sequence number of the ledger.

CLOSED_AT.name: CLOSED_AT
CLOSED_AT.comment: Timestamp when the ledger was closed and committed to the network. Ledgers are expected to close ~every 5 seconds.

ACCOUNT.name: ACCOUNT
ACCOUNT.comment: The account involved in the operation.

OPERATION_COUNT.name: OPERATION_COUNT
OPERATION_COUNT.comment: The total number of successful operations applied to this ledger.

MEMO_TYPE.name: MEMO_TYPE
MEMO_TYPE.comment: Type of memo attached to transaction.

FEE_CHARGED.name: FEE_CHARGED
FEE_CHARGED.comment: Actual fee charged for the operation.

INNER_TRANSACTION_HASH.name: INNER_TRANSACTION_HASH
INNER_TRANSACTION_HASH.comment: A transaction hash of a transaction wrapped with its signatures for fee-bump transactions

BATCH_ID.name: BATCH_ID
BATCH_ID.comment: String representation of the run id for a given DAG in Airflow. Takes the form of "scheduled__<batch_end_date>-<dag_alias>". Batch ids are unique to the batch and help with monitoring and rerun capabilities.

BATCH_RUN_DATE.name: BATCH_RUN_DATE
BATCH_RUN_DATE.comment: The start date for the batch interval. When taken with the date in the batch_id, the date represents the interval of ledgers processed. The batch run date can be seen as a proxy of closed_at for a ledger.

BATCH_INSERT_TS.name: BATCH_INSERT_TS
BATCH_INSERT_TS.comment: The timestamp in UTC when a batch of records was inserted into the database. This field can help identify if a batch executed in real time or as part of a backfill. The timestamp should not be used during ad hoc analysis and is useful for data engineering purposes.

TX_ENVELOPE.name: TX_ENVELOPE
TX_ENVELOPE.comment: A base-64 encoded XDR blob of the tx envelope (transaction and its signatures)

TX_RESULT.name: TX_RESULT
TX_RESULT.comment: A base-64 encoded XDR blob of the tx result

TX_META.name: TX_META
TX_META.comment: A base-64 encoded XDR blob of the tx meta

TX_FEE_META.name: TX_FEE_META
TX_FEE_META.comment: A base-64 encoded XDR blob of the tx fee meta

NON_REFUNDABLE_RESOURCE_FEE_CHARGED.name: NON_REFUNDABLE_RESOURCE_FEE_CHARGED
NON_REFUNDABLE_RESOURCE_FEE_CHARGED.comment: The amount of the resource fee charged for the transaction that is non-refundable.

REFUNDABLE_RESOURCE_FEE_CHARGED.name: REFUNDABLE_RESOURCE_FEE_CHARGED
REFUNDABLE_RESOURCE_FEE_CHARGED.comment: The amount of the resource fee charged for the transaction that is refundable.

RENT_FEE_CHARGED.name: RENT_FEE_CHARGED
RENT_FEE_CHARGED.comment: The fee charged for renting resources on the network, such as storage space for data.

TX_SIGNERS.name: TX_SIGNERS
TX_SIGNERS.comment: The public keys of the signers who authorized the transaction.

REFUNDABLE_FEE.name: REFUNDABLE_FEE
REFUNDABLE_FEE.comment: The portion of the transaction fee that is refundable under certain conditions.

FACT_TRANSACTIONS_ID.name: FACT_TRANSACTIONS_ID
FACT_TRANSACTIONS_ID.comment: Unique primary key for the dimension table, used as the main identifier for each record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: Timestamp when this record was inserted.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: Timestamp when this record was last modified.

metadata.name: FACT_ACCOUNTS
metadata.comment: Core fact table containing all Stellar account information and their current states

ACCOUNT_ID.name: ACCOUNT_ID
ACCOUNT_ID.comment: The address of the account. The address is the account's public key encoded in base32. All account addresses start with a 'G'.

BALANCE.name: BALANCE
BALANCE.comment: The number of units of XLM held by the account.

BUYING_LIABILITIES.name: BUYING_LIABILITIES
BUYING_LIABILITIES.comment: The sum of all buy offers owned by this account for XLM only.

SELLING_LIABILITIES.name: SELLING_LIABILITIES
SELLING_LIABILITIES.comment: The sum of all sell offers owned by this account for XLM only.

SEQUENCE_NUMBER.name: SEQUENCE_NUMBER
SEQUENCE_NUMBER.comment: The account's current sequence number. The sequence number controls operations applied to an account. Operations must submit a unique sequence number that is incremented by 1 in order to apply the operation to the account so that account changes will not collide within a ledger.

NUM_SUBENTRIES.name: NUM_SUBENTRIES
NUM_SUBENTRIES.comment: The total number of ledger entries connected to this account. Ledger entries include: trustlines, offers, signers, and data entries. (Claimable balances are counted under sponsoring entries, not subentries). Any newly created trustline, offer, signer or data entry will increase the number of subentries by 1. Accounts may have up to 1,000 subentries.

INFLATION_DESTINATION.name: INFLATION_DESTINATION
INFLATION_DESTINATION.comment: Deprecated: The account address to receive an inflation payment when they are disbursed on the network.

FLAGS.name: FLAGS
FLAGS.comment: Denotes the enabling and disabling of certain asset issuer privileges.

HOME_DOMAIN.name: HOME_DOMAIN
HOME_DOMAIN.comment: The domain that hosts this account's stellar.toml file.

MASTER_WEIGHT.name: MASTER_WEIGHT
MASTER_WEIGHT.comment: The weight of the master key, which is the private key for this account. If a master key is '0', the account is locked and cannot be used.

THRESHOLD_LOW.name: THRESHOLD_LOW
THRESHOLD_LOW.comment: The sum of the weight of all signatures that sign a transaction for the low threshold operation. The weight must exceed the set threshold for the operation to succeed.

THRESHOLD_MEDIUM.name: THRESHOLD_MEDIUM
THRESHOLD_MEDIUM.comment: The sum of the weight of all signatures that sign a transaction for the medium threshold operation. The weight must exceed the set threshold for the operation to succeed.

THRESHOLD_HIGH.name: THRESHOLD_HIGH
THRESHOLD_HIGH.comment: The sum of the weight of all signatures that sign a transaction for the high threshold operation. The weight must exceed the set threshold for the operation to succeed.

LAST_MODIFIED_LEDGER.name: LAST_MODIFIED_LEDGER
LAST_MODIFIED_LEDGER.comment: The ledger sequence number when the ledger entry was last modified. Deletions do not count as a modification and will report the prior modification sequence number.

LEDGER_ENTRY_CHANGE.name: LEDGER_ENTRY_CHANGE
LEDGER_ENTRY_CHANGE.comment: Code that describes the ledger entry change type that was applied to the ledger entry.

DELETED.name: DELETED
DELETED.comment: Indicates whether the ledger entry (account, claimable balance, trust line, offer, liquidity pool) has been deleted or not. Once an entry is deleted, it cannot be recovered.

SPONSOR.name: SPONSOR
SPONSOR.comment: The account address of the sponsor who is paying the reserves for this ledger entry.

NUM_SPONSORED.name: NUM_SPONSORED
NUM_SPONSORED.comment: The number of reserves sponsored for this account (meaning another account is paying for the minimum balance). Sponsored entries do not incur any reserve requirement on the account that owns the entry.

NUM_SPONSORING.name: NUM_SPONSORING
NUM_SPONSORING.comment: The number of reserves sponsored by this account. Entries sponsored by this account incur a reserve requirement.

SEQUENCE_LEDGER.name: SEQUENCE_LEDGER
SEQUENCE_LEDGER.comment: The unsigned 32-bit ledger number of the sequence number's age.

SEQUENCE_TIME.name: SEQUENCE_TIME
SEQUENCE_TIME.comment: The UNIX timestamp of the sequence number's age.

CLOSED_AT.name: CLOSED_AT
CLOSED_AT.comment: Timestamp when the ledger was closed and committed to the network. Ledgers are expected to close ~every 5 seconds.

LEDGER_SEQUENCE.name: LEDGER_SEQUENCE
LEDGER_SEQUENCE.comment: The sequence number of the ledger.

FACT_ACCOUNTS_ID.name: FACT_ACCOUNTS_ID
FACT_ACCOUNTS_ID.comment: Unique primary key for the dimension table, used as the main identifier for each record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: Timestamp when this record was inserted.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: Timestamp when this record was last modified.

metadata.name: FACT_OPERATIONS
metadata.comment: A comprehensive view of all operations executed on the Stellar network, including transaction details, operation types, and their outcomes.

CLOSED_AT.name: CLOSED_AT
CLOSED_AT.comment: Timestamp when the ledger was closed and committed to the network. Ledgers are expected to close ~every 5 seconds.

ACCOUNT.name: ACCOUNT
ACCOUNT.comment: The account involved in the operation.

AMOUNT.name: AMOUNT
AMOUNT.comment: Float representation of the amount of an asset sent/offered/etc.

ASSET_CODE.name: ASSET_CODE
ASSET_CODE.comment: The 4 or 12 character code representation of the asset on the network.

ASSET_ISSUER.name: ASSET_ISSUER
ASSET_ISSUER.comment: The account address of the original asset issuer that created the asset.

ASSET_TYPE.name: ASSET_TYPE
ASSET_TYPE.comment: The identifier for type of asset code, can be an alphanumeric with 4 characters, 12 characters or the native asset to the network, XLM.

ASSET_ID.name: ASSET_ID
ASSET_ID.comment: The Farm Hash encoding of Asset Code + Asset Issuer + Asset Type. This field is optimized for cross table joins since integer joins are less expensive than the original asset id components.

AUTHORIZE.name: AUTHORIZE
AUTHORIZE.comment: Indicates whether the trustline is authorized.

BALANCE_ID.name: BALANCE_ID
BALANCE_ID.comment: The balance id of the claimable balance.

BUYING_ASSET_CODE.name: BUYING_ASSET_CODE
BUYING_ASSET_CODE.comment: The 4 or 12 character code representation of the asset being bought.

BUYING_ASSET_ISSUER.name: BUYING_ASSET_ISSUER
BUYING_ASSET_ISSUER.comment: The account address of the original asset issuer for the buying asset.

BUYING_ASSET_TYPE.name: BUYING_ASSET_TYPE
BUYING_ASSET_TYPE.comment: The identifier for type of buying asset code.

BUYING_ASSET_ID.name: BUYING_ASSET_ID
BUYING_ASSET_ID.comment: The identifier of the asset being bought.

FROM_ACCOUNT.name: FROM_ACCOUNT
FROM_ACCOUNT.comment: The account address from which the payment/contract originates.

FUNDER.name: FUNDER
FUNDER.comment: The account address that funds a new account.

HIGH_THRESHOLD.name: HIGH_THRESHOLD
HIGH_THRESHOLD.comment: The sum of the weight of all signatures that sign a transaction for the high threshold operation. The weight must exceed the set threshold for the operation to succeed.

HOME_DOMAIN.name: HOME_DOMAIN
HOME_DOMAIN.comment: The domain that hosts this account's stellar.toml file.

INFLATION_DEST.name: INFLATION_DEST
INFLATION_DEST.comment: Deprecated: The account address to receive an inflation payment when they are disbursed on the network.

INTO_ACCOUNT.name: INTO_ACCOUNT
INTO_ACCOUNT.comment: The account address receiving the deleted account's lumens.

LIMIT_AMOUNT.name: LIMIT_AMOUNT
LIMIT_AMOUNT.comment: The upper bound amount of an asset that an account can hold.

LOW_THRESHOLD.name: LOW_THRESHOLD
LOW_THRESHOLD.comment: The sum of the weight of all signatures that sign a transaction for the low threshold operation. The weight must exceed the set threshold for the operation to succeed.

MASTER_KEY_WEIGHT.name: MASTER_KEY_WEIGHT
MASTER_KEY_WEIGHT.comment: The weight of the master key, which is the private key for this account. If a master key is '0', the account is locked and cannot be used.

MED_THRESHOLD.name: MED_THRESHOLD
MED_THRESHOLD.comment: The sum of the weight of all signatures that sign a transaction for the medium threshold operation. The weight must exceed the set threshold for the operation to succeed.

NAME.name: NAME
NAME.comment: The key for a data entry in manage data operations.

OFFER_ID.name: OFFER_ID
OFFER_ID.comment: The unique id for the offer.

PRICE.name: PRICE
PRICE.comment: The ratio of selling asset to buying asset.

SELLING_ASSET_CODE.name: SELLING_ASSET_CODE
SELLING_ASSET_CODE.comment: The 4 or 12 character code representation of the asset being sold.

SELLING_ASSET_ISSUER.name: SELLING_ASSET_ISSUER
SELLING_ASSET_ISSUER.comment: The account address of the original asset issuer for the selling asset.

SELLING_ASSET_TYPE.name: SELLING_ASSET_TYPE
SELLING_ASSET_TYPE.comment: The identifier for type of selling asset code.

SELLING_ASSET_ID.name: SELLING_ASSET_ID
SELLING_ASSET_ID.comment: The identifier of the asset being sold.

SET_FLAGS.name: SET_FLAGS
SET_FLAGS.comment: Array of numeric values of flags set for a trustline.

SET_FLAGS_S.name: SET_FLAGS_S
SET_FLAGS_S.comment: Array of string values of flags set for a trustline.

SIGNER_WEIGHT.name: SIGNER_WEIGHT
SIGNER_WEIGHT.comment: The weight of the new signer.

SOURCE_AMOUNT.name: SOURCE_AMOUNT
SOURCE_AMOUNT.comment: The originating amount sent designated in the source asset.

SOURCE_ASSET_CODE.name: SOURCE_ASSET_CODE
SOURCE_ASSET_CODE.comment: The 4 or 12 character code representation of the source asset.

SOURCE_ASSET_ISSUER.name: SOURCE_ASSET_ISSUER
SOURCE_ASSET_ISSUER.comment: The account address of the original source asset issuer.

SOURCE_ASSET_TYPE.name: SOURCE_ASSET_TYPE
SOURCE_ASSET_TYPE.comment: The identifier for type of source asset code.

SOURCE_ASSET_ID.name: SOURCE_ASSET_ID
SOURCE_ASSET_ID.comment: The identifier of the source asset.

SOURCE_MAX.name: SOURCE_MAX
SOURCE_MAX.comment: The maximum amount to be sent in source asset.

STARTING_BALANCE.name: STARTING_BALANCE
STARTING_BALANCE.comment: The amount of XLM sent to newly created account.

TO_ACCOUNT.name: TO_ACCOUNT
TO_ACCOUNT.comment: The address of the account receiving payment funds.

VALUE.name: VALUE
VALUE.comment: The value for a data entry in manage data operations.

CLEAR_FLAGS.name: CLEAR_FLAGS
CLEAR_FLAGS.comment: Array of numeric values of flags cleared for a trustline.

CLEAR_FLAGS_S.name: CLEAR_FLAGS_S
CLEAR_FLAGS_S.comment: Array of string values of flags cleared for a trustline.

DESTINATION_MIN.name: DESTINATION_MIN
DESTINATION_MIN.comment: The minimum amount to be received in destination asset.

BUMP_TO.name: BUMP_TO
BUMP_TO.comment: The new desired value of the source account's sequence number.

SPONSOR.name: SPONSOR
SPONSOR.comment: The account address of the sponsor who is paying the reserves for this ledger entry.

SPONSORED_ID.name: SPONSORED_ID
SPONSORED_ID.comment: The account address of the account being sponsored.

BEGIN_SPONSOR.name: BEGIN_SPONSOR
BEGIN_SPONSOR.comment: The account address that initiated the sponsorship.

AUTHORIZE_TO_MAINTAIN_LIABILITIES.name: AUTHORIZE_TO_MAINTAIN_LIABILITIES
AUTHORIZE_TO_MAINTAIN_LIABILITIES.comment: Indicates whether the trustline is authorized for maintaining liabilities.

CLAWBACK_ENABLED.name: CLAWBACK_ENABLED
CLAWBACK_ENABLED.comment: Indicates whether the asset can be clawed back by the issuer.

LIQUIDITY_POOL_ID.name: LIQUIDITY_POOL_ID
LIQUIDITY_POOL_ID.comment: Unique identifier for a liquidity pool. There cannot be duplicate pools for the same asset pair. Once a pool has been created for the asset pair, another cannot be created.

RESERVE_A_ASSET_TYPE.name: RESERVE_A_ASSET_TYPE
RESERVE_A_ASSET_TYPE.comment: The type of the first reserve asset.

RESERVE_A_ASSET_ID.name: RESERVE_A_ASSET_ID
RESERVE_A_ASSET_ID.comment: The identifier of the first reserve asset.

RESERVE_A_ASSET_CODE.name: RESERVE_A_ASSET_CODE
RESERVE_A_ASSET_CODE.comment: The code of the first reserve asset.

RESERVE_A_ASSET_ISSUER.name: RESERVE_A_ASSET_ISSUER
RESERVE_A_ASSET_ISSUER.comment: The issuer of the first reserve asset.

RESERVE_A_MAX_AMOUNT.name: RESERVE_A_MAX_AMOUNT
RESERVE_A_MAX_AMOUNT.comment: Maximum amount for first reserve asset deposit.

RESERVE_A_DEPOSIT_AMOUNT.name: RESERVE_A_DEPOSIT_AMOUNT
RESERVE_A_DEPOSIT_AMOUNT.comment: Actual amount deposited for first reserve asset.

RESERVE_B_ASSET_TYPE.name: RESERVE_B_ASSET_TYPE
RESERVE_B_ASSET_TYPE.comment: The type of the second reserve asset.

RESERVE_B_ASSET_ID.name: RESERVE_B_ASSET_ID
RESERVE_B_ASSET_ID.comment: The identifier of the second reserve asset.

RESERVE_B_ASSET_CODE.name: RESERVE_B_ASSET_CODE
RESERVE_B_ASSET_CODE.comment: The code of the second reserve asset.

RESERVE_B_ASSET_ISSUER.name: RESERVE_B_ASSET_ISSUER
RESERVE_B_ASSET_ISSUER.comment: The issuer of the second reserve asset.

RESERVE_B_MAX_AMOUNT.name: RESERVE_B_MAX_AMOUNT
RESERVE_B_MAX_AMOUNT.comment: Maximum amount for second reserve asset deposit.

RESERVE_B_DEPOSIT_AMOUNT.name: RESERVE_B_DEPOSIT_AMOUNT
RESERVE_B_DEPOSIT_AMOUNT.comment: Actual amount deposited for second reserve asset.

MIN_PRICE_R.name: MIN_PRICE_R
MIN_PRICE_R.comment: A fractional representation of the prices of the two assets in a pool. The n is the numerator (value of asset a) and the d is the denominator (value of asset b).

MAX_PRICE_R.name: MAX_PRICE_R
MAX_PRICE_R.comment: A fractional representation of the prices of the two assets in a pool. The n is the numerator (value of asset a) and the d is the denominator (value of asset b).

SHARES_RECEIVED.name: SHARES_RECEIVED
SHARES_RECEIVED.comment: Number of pool shares received for deposit.

RESERVE_A_MIN_AMOUNT.name: RESERVE_A_MIN_AMOUNT
RESERVE_A_MIN_AMOUNT.comment: Minimum withdrawal amount for first reserve asset.

RESERVE_B_MIN_AMOUNT.name: RESERVE_B_MIN_AMOUNT
RESERVE_B_MIN_AMOUNT.comment: Minimum withdrawal amount for second reserve asset.

SHARES.name: SHARES
SHARES.comment: Number of shares withdrawn from pool.

RESERVE_A_WITHDRAW_AMOUNT.name: RESERVE_A_WITHDRAW_AMOUNT
RESERVE_A_WITHDRAW_AMOUNT.comment: Actual withdrawal amount for first reserve asset.

RESERVE_B_WITHDRAW_AMOUNT.name: RESERVE_B_WITHDRAW_AMOUNT
RESERVE_B_WITHDRAW_AMOUNT.comment: Actual withdrawal amount for second reserve asset.

OP_SOURCE_ACCOUNT_MUXED.name: OP_SOURCE_ACCOUNT_MUXED
OP_SOURCE_ACCOUNT_MUXED.comment: Muxed source account for the operation.

TRANSACTION_ID.name: TRANSACTION_ID
TRANSACTION_ID.comment: The transaction identifier containing this operation.

TYPE.name: TYPE
TYPE.comment: The mechanism that calculates pricing and division of shares for the pool. With the initial AMM rollout, the only type of liquidity pool allowed to be created is a constant product pool.

LEDGER_SEQUENCE.name: LEDGER_SEQUENCE
LEDGER_SEQUENCE.comment: The sequence number of the ledger.

BATCH_ID.name: BATCH_ID
BATCH_ID.comment: String representation of the run id for a given DAG in Airflow. Takes the form of "scheduled__<batch_end_date>-<dag_alias>". Batch ids are unique to the batch and help with monitoring and rerun capabilities.

BATCH_RUN_DATE.name: BATCH_RUN_DATE
BATCH_RUN_DATE.comment: The start date for the batch interval. When taken with the date in the batch_id, the date represents the interval of ledgers processed. The batch run date can be seen as a proxy of closed_at for a ledger.

BATCH_INSERT_TS.name: BATCH_INSERT_TS
BATCH_INSERT_TS.comment: The timestamp in UTC when a batch of records was inserted into the database. This field can help identify if a batch executed in real time or as part of a backfill. The timestamp should not be used during ad hoc analysis and is useful for data engineering purposes.

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: Timestamp when this record was inserted.

metadata.name: DIM_LABELS
metadata.comment: The labels table is a store of one-to-one address identifiers, or an address name. Labels are broken out into a "type" (such as cex, dex, dapp, games, etc.) and a "subtype" (ex: contract_deployer, hot_wallet, token_contract, etc.) in order to help classify each address name into similar groups. Our labels are sourced from many different places, but can primarily be grouped into two categories: automatic and manual. Automatic labels are continuously labeled based on certain criteria, such as a known contract deploying another contract, behavior based algorithms for finding deposit wallets, and consistent data pulls of custom protocol APIs. Manual labels are done periodically to find addresses that cannot be found programmatically such as finding new protocol addresses, centralized exchange hot wallets, or trending addresses. Labels can also be added by our community by using our add-a-label tool (https://science.flipsidecrypto.xyz/add-a-label/) or on-chain with near (https://near.social/lord1.near/widget/Form) and are reviewed by our labels team. A label can be removed by our labels team if it is found to be incorrect or no longer relevant; this generally will only happen for mislabeled deposit wallets.

metadata.name: FACT_LIQUIDITY_POOLS
metadata.comment: Fact table containing liquidity pool data including pool details, asset pairs, and metrics.

LIQUIDITY_POOL_ID.name: LIQUIDITY_POOL_ID
LIQUIDITY_POOL_ID.comment: Unique identifier for a liquidity pool. There cannot be duplicate pools for the same asset pair. Once a pool has been created for the asset pair, another cannot be created.

CLOSED_AT.name: CLOSED_AT
CLOSED_AT.comment: Timestamp when the ledger was closed and committed to the network. Ledgers are expected to close ~every 5 seconds.

TYPE.name: TYPE
TYPE.comment: The mechanism that calculates pricing and division of shares for the pool. With the initial AMM rollout, the only type of liquidity pool allowed to be created is a constant product pool.

FEE.name: FEE
FEE.comment: The number of basis points charged as a percentage of the trade in order to complete the transaction. The fees earned on all trades are divided amongst pool shareholders and distributed as an incentive to keep money in the pools.

TRUSTLINE_COUNT.name: TRUSTLINE_COUNT
TRUSTLINE_COUNT.comment: Total number of accounts with trustlines authorized to the pool. To create a trustline, an account must trust both base assets before trusting a pool with the asset pair.

POOL_SHARE_COUNT.name: POOL_SHARE_COUNT
POOL_SHARE_COUNT.comment: Participation in a liquidity pool is represented by a pool share.

ASSET_A_TYPE.name: ASSET_A_TYPE
ASSET_A_TYPE.comment: The identifier for type of asset code, can be an alphanumeric with 4 characters, 12 characters or the native asset to the network, XLM.

ASSET_A_CODE.name: ASSET_A_CODE
ASSET_A_CODE.comment: The 4 or 12 character code representation of the asset on the network.

ASSET_A_ISSUER.name: ASSET_A_ISSUER
ASSET_A_ISSUER.comment: The account address of the original asset issuer that created the asset.

ASSET_A_ID.name: ASSET_A_ID
ASSET_A_ID.comment: The Farm Hash encoding of Asset Code + Asset Issuer + Asset Type. This field is optimized for cross table joins since integer joins are less expensive than the original asset id components.

ASSET_A_AMOUNT.name: ASSET_A_AMOUNT
ASSET_A_AMOUNT.comment: The raw number of tokens locked in the pool for one of the two asset pairs in the liquidity pool.

ASSET_B_TYPE.name: ASSET_B_TYPE
ASSET_B_TYPE.comment: The identifier for type of asset code, can be an alphanumeric with 4 characters, 12 characters or the native asset to the network, XLM.

ASSET_B_CODE.name: ASSET_B_CODE
ASSET_B_CODE.comment: The 4 or 12 character code representation of the asset on the network.

ASSET_B_ISSUER.name: ASSET_B_ISSUER
ASSET_B_ISSUER.comment: The account address of the original asset issuer that created the asset.

ASSET_B_ID.name: ASSET_B_ID
ASSET_B_ID.comment: The Farm Hash encoding of Asset Code + Asset Issuer + Asset Type. This field is optimized for cross table joins since integer joins are less expensive than the original asset id components.

ASSET_B_AMOUNT.name: ASSET_B_AMOUNT
ASSET_B_AMOUNT.comment: The raw number of tokens locked in the pool for one of the two asset pairs in the liquidity pool.

LAST_MODIFIED_LEDGER.name: LAST_MODIFIED_LEDGER
LAST_MODIFIED_LEDGER.comment: The ledger sequence number when the ledger entry was last modified. Deletions do not count as a modification and will report the prior modification sequence number.

LEDGER_ENTRY_CHANGE.name: LEDGER_ENTRY_CHANGE
LEDGER_ENTRY_CHANGE.comment: Code that describes the ledger entry change type that was applied to the ledger entry.

DELETED.name: DELETED
DELETED.comment: Indicates whether the ledger entry (account, claimable balance, trust line, offer, liquidity pool) has been deleted or not. Once an entry is deleted, it cannot be recovered.

BATCH_ID.name: BATCH_ID
BATCH_ID.comment: String representation of the run id for a given DAG in Airflow. Takes the form of "scheduled__<batch_end_date>-<dag_alias>". Batch ids are unique to the batch and help with monitoring and rerun capabilities.

BATCH_RUN_DATE.name: BATCH_RUN_DATE
BATCH_RUN_DATE.comment: The start date for the batch interval. When taken with the date in the batch_id, the date represents the interval of ledgers processed. The batch run date can be seen as a proxy of closed_at for a ledger.

BATCH_INSERT_TS.name: BATCH_INSERT_TS
BATCH_INSERT_TS.comment: The timestamp in UTC when a batch of records was inserted into the database. This field can help identify if a batch executed in real time or as part of a backfill. The timestamp should not be used during ad hoc analysis and is useful for data engineering purposes.

LEDGER_SEQUENCE.name: LEDGER_SEQUENCE
LEDGER_SEQUENCE.comment: The sequence number of the ledger.

FACT_LIQUIDITY_POOLS_ID.name: FACT_LIQUIDITY_POOLS_ID
FACT_LIQUIDITY_POOLS_ID.comment: Unique primary key for the dimension table, used as the main identifier for each record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: Timestamp when this record was inserted.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: Timestamp when this record was last modified.

metadata.name: FACT_TRADES
metadata.comment: A fact table containing trade execution details from both the decentralized exchange and liquidity pools.

HISTORY_OPERATION_ID.name: HISTORY_OPERATION_ID
HISTORY_OPERATION_ID.comment: The operation id associated with the executed trade. The total amount traded in an operation can be broken up into multiple smaller trades spread across multiple orders by multiple parties.

TRADE_ORDER.name: TRADE_ORDER
TRADE_ORDER.comment: The sequential number assigned the portion of a trade that is executed within a operation. The history_operation_id and order number together represent a unique trade segment.

LEDGER_CLOSED_AT.name: LEDGER_CLOSED_AT
LEDGER_CLOSED_AT.comment: Timestamp when the ledger was closed and committed to the network. Ledgers are expected to close ~every 5 seconds.

SELLING_ACCOUNT_ADDRESS.name: SELLING_ACCOUNT_ADDRESS
SELLING_ACCOUNT_ADDRESS.comment: The account address of the selling party.

SELLING_ASSET_CODE.name: SELLING_ASSET_CODE
SELLING_ASSET_CODE.comment: The 4 or 12 character code representation of the asset being sold.

SELLING_ASSET_ISSUER.name: SELLING_ASSET_ISSUER
SELLING_ASSET_ISSUER.comment: The account address of the original asset issuer for the selling asset.

SELLING_ASSET_TYPE.name: SELLING_ASSET_TYPE
SELLING_ASSET_TYPE.comment: The identifier for type of selling asset code.

SELLING_ASSET_ID.name: SELLING_ASSET_ID
SELLING_ASSET_ID.comment: The identifier of the asset being sold.

SELLING_AMOUNT.name: SELLING_AMOUNT
SELLING_AMOUNT.comment: The amount of sold asset that was moved from the seller account to the buyer account, reported in terms of the sold amount.

BUYING_ACCOUNT_ADDRESS.name: BUYING_ACCOUNT_ADDRESS
BUYING_ACCOUNT_ADDRESS.comment: The account address of the buying party.

BUYING_ASSET_CODE.name: BUYING_ASSET_CODE
BUYING_ASSET_CODE.comment: The 4 or 12 character code representation of the asset being bought.

BUYING_ASSET_ISSUER.name: BUYING_ASSET_ISSUER
BUYING_ASSET_ISSUER.comment: The account address of the original asset issuer for the buying asset.

BUYING_ASSET_TYPE.name: BUYING_ASSET_TYPE
BUYING_ASSET_TYPE.comment: The identifier for type of buying asset code.

BUYING_ASSET_ID.name: BUYING_ASSET_ID
BUYING_ASSET_ID.comment: The identifier of the asset being bought.

BUYING_AMOUNT.name: BUYING_AMOUNT
BUYING_AMOUNT.comment: The amount of purchased asset that was moved from the seller account into the buying account, reported in terms of the bought asset.

PRICE_N.name: PRICE_N
PRICE_N.comment: The price ratio of the sold asset: bought asset. When taken with price_d, the price can be calculated by price_n/price_d.

PRICE_D.name: PRICE_D
PRICE_D.comment: The price ratio of the sold asset: bought asset. When taken with price_n, the price can be calculated by price_n/price_d.

SELLING_OFFER_ID.name: SELLING_OFFER_ID
SELLING_OFFER_ID.comment: The offer ID in the orderbook of the selling offer. If this offer was immediately and fully consumed, this will be a synthetic ID.

BUYING_OFFER_ID.name: BUYING_OFFER_ID
BUYING_OFFER_ID.comment: The offer ID in the orderbook of the buying offer. If this offer was immediately and fully consumed, this will be a synthetic ID.

SELLING_LIQUIDITY_POOL_ID.name: SELLING_LIQUIDITY_POOL_ID
SELLING_LIQUIDITY_POOL_ID.comment: The unique identifier for a liquidity pool if the trade was executed against a liquidity pool instead of the orderbook.

LIQUIDITY_POOL_FEE.name: LIQUIDITY_POOL_FEE
LIQUIDITY_POOL_FEE.comment: The percentage fee (in basis points) of the total fee collected by the liquidity pool for executing the trade.

TRADE_TYPE.name: TRADE_TYPE
TRADE_TYPE.comment: Indicates whether the trade was executed against the orderbook (decentralized exchange) or liquidity pool.

ROUNDING_SLIPPAGE.name: ROUNDING_SLIPPAGE
ROUNDING_SLIPPAGE.comment: Applies to liquidity pool trades only. With fractional amounts of an asset traded, the network must round a fraction to the nearest whole number. This can cause the trade to "slip" price by a percentage compared with the original offer. Rounding slippage reports the percentage that dust trades slip before executing.

SELLER_IS_EXACT.name: SELLER_IS_EXACT
SELLER_IS_EXACT.comment: Indicates whether the buying or selling party trade was impacted by rounding slippage. If true, the buyer was impacted. If false, the seller was impacted.

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: Timestamp when this record was inserted.

FACT_TRADES_ID.name: FACT_TRADES_ID
FACT_TRADES_ID.comment: Unique primary key for the dimension table, used as the main identifier for each record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: Timestamp when this record was inserted.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: Timestamp when this record was last modified.

metadata.name: EZ_OPERATIONS
metadata.comment: A comprehensive view combining operations with their associated transaction and ledger data.

AMOUNT.name: AMOUNT
AMOUNT.comment: Float representation of the amount of an asset sent/offered/etc.

ASSET_CODE.name: ASSET_CODE
ASSET_CODE.comment: The 4 or 12 character code representation of the asset on the network.

ASSET_ISSUER.name: ASSET_ISSUER
ASSET_ISSUER.comment: The account address of the original asset issuer that created the asset.

ASSET_TYPE.name: ASSET_TYPE
ASSET_TYPE.comment: The identifier for type of asset code, can be an alphanumeric with 4 characters, 12 characters or the native asset to the network, XLM.

TYPE.name: TYPE
TYPE.comment: The mechanism that calculates pricing and division of shares for the pool. With the initial AMM rollout, the only type of liquidity pool allowed to be created is a constant product pool.

CLOSED_AT.name: CLOSED_AT
CLOSED_AT.comment: Timestamp when the ledger was closed and committed to the network. Ledgers are expected to close ~every 5 seconds.

LEDGER_SEQUENCE.name: LEDGER_SEQUENCE
LEDGER_SEQUENCE.comment: The sequence number of the ledger.

NON_REFUNDABLE_RESOURCE_FEE_CHARGED.name: NON_REFUNDABLE_RESOURCE_FEE_CHARGED
NON_REFUNDABLE_RESOURCE_FEE_CHARGED.comment: The amount of the resource fee charged for the transaction that is non-refundable.

REFUNDABLE_RESOURCE_FEE_CHARGED.name: REFUNDABLE_RESOURCE_FEE_CHARGED
REFUNDABLE_RESOURCE_FEE_CHARGED.comment: The amount of the resource fee charged for the transaction that is refundable.

RENT_FEE_CHARGED.name: RENT_FEE_CHARGED
RENT_FEE_CHARGED.comment: The fee charged for renting resources on the network, such as storage space for data.

TX_SIGNERS.name: TX_SIGNERS
TX_SIGNERS.comment: The public keys of the signers who authorized the transaction.

REFUNDABLE_FEE.name: REFUNDABLE_FEE
REFUNDABLE_FEE.comment: The portion of the transaction fee that is refundable under certain conditions.

FACT_OPERATIONS_ID.name: FACT_OPERATIONS_ID
FACT_OPERATIONS_ID.comment: Unique primary key for the dimension table, used as the main identifier for each record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: Timestamp when this record was inserted.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: Timestamp when this record was last modified.

metadata.name: FACT_LEDGERS
metadata.comment: Fact table containing information about ledgers on the Stellar network.

SEQUENCE.name: SEQUENCE
SEQUENCE.comment: The sequence number that corresponds to the individual ledgers. As ledgers are written to the network, the sequence is incremented by 1

LEDGER_HASH.name: LEDGER_HASH
LEDGER_HASH.comment: The hex-encoded SHA-256 hash that represents the ledger's XDR-encoded form.

PREVIOUS_LEDGER_HASH.name: PREVIOUS_LEDGER_HASH
PREVIOUS_LEDGER_HASH.comment: The hex-encoded SHA-256 hash of the ledger that immediately precedes this ledger.

TRANSACTION_COUNT.name: TRANSACTION_COUNT
TRANSACTION_COUNT.comment: The number of successful transactions submitted and completed by the network in this ledger.

OPERATION_COUNT.name: OPERATION_COUNT
OPERATION_COUNT.comment: The total number of successful operations applied to this ledger.

CLOSED_AT.name: CLOSED_AT
CLOSED_AT.comment: Timestamp when the ledger was closed and committed to the network. Ledgers are expected to close ~every 5 seconds.

TOTAL_COINS.name: TOTAL_COINS
TOTAL_COINS.comment: Total number of lumens in circulation

FEE_POOL.name: FEE_POOL
FEE_POOL.comment: The sum of all transaction fees

BASE_FEE.name: BASE_FEE
BASE_FEE.comment: The fee (in stroops) the network charges per operation in a transaction for the given ledger. The minimum base fee is 100, with the ability to increase if transaction demand exceeds ledger capacity. When this occurs, the ledger enters surge pricing.

BASE_RESERVE.name: BASE_RESERVE
BASE_RESERVE.comment: The reserve (in stroops) the network requires an account to retain as a minimum balance in order to be a valid account on the network. The current minimum reserve is 10 XLM.

MAX_TX_SET_SIZE.name: MAX_TX_SET_SIZE
MAX_TX_SET_SIZE.comment: The maximum number of operations that Stellar validator nodes have agreed to process in a given ledger. Since Protocol 11, ledger capacity has been measured in operations rather than transactions.

PROTOCOL_VERSION.name: PROTOCOL_VERSION
PROTOCOL_VERSION.comment: The protocol verstion that the Stellar network was running when this ledger was committed. Protocol versions are released ~every 6 months.

SUCCESSFUL_TRANSACTION_COUNT.name: SUCCESSFUL_TRANSACTION_COUNT
SUCCESSFUL_TRANSACTION_COUNT.comment: The number of successful transactions submitted and completed by the network in this ledger

FAILED_TRANSACTION_COUNT.name: FAILED_TRANSACTION_COUNT
FAILED_TRANSACTION_COUNT.comment: The number of failed transactions submitted to the network in this ledger. The transaction was still paid for but contained an error that prevented it from executing

TX_SET_OPERATION_COUNT.name: TX_SET_OPERATION_COUNT
TX_SET_OPERATION_COUNT.comment: The total number of operations in the transaction set for this ledger, including failed transactions.

SOROBAN_FEE_WRITE_1KB.name: SOROBAN_FEE_WRITE_1KB
SOROBAN_FEE_WRITE_1KB.comment: The fee associated with writing 1KB of data to the Soroban ledger.The write fee is dynamic fee that grows linearly with the size of the ledger. The write fee for a given ledger is recorded in the ledger header and has a direct relationship to the bucketlist size of the ledger db.

NODE_ID.name: NODE_ID
NODE_ID.comment: The id of winning validator node which is allowed to write transaction set to the network. The winning validator is decided by the network.

SIGNATURE.name: SIGNATURE
SIGNATURE.comment: The signing hash of the validator node which writes the transaction set to the network.This signature ensures the integrity and authenticity of the ledger, confirming that it has not been tampered with.

TOTAL_BYTE_SIZE_OF_BUCKET_LIST.name: TOTAL_BYTE_SIZE_OF_BUCKET_LIST
TOTAL_BYTE_SIZE_OF_BUCKET_LIST.comment: Total size in bytes of the bucket list

FACT_LEDGERS_ID.name: FACT_LEDGERS_ID
FACT_LEDGERS_ID.comment: Unique primary key for the dimension table, used as the main identifier for each record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: Timestamp when this record was inserted.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: Timestamp when this record was last modified.

metadata.name: EZ_DECODED_EVENT_LOGS
metadata.comment:
'For information on how to submit a contract for decoding, as well as how ABIs are sourced, please visit [here](https://science.flipsidecrypto.xyz/abi-requestor/). This model contains decoded event logs for contracts that we have an ABI for. Please note, this table does not include all event logs, only those that we have an ABI for. The `decoded_log` column is the easiest place to query decoded data. It is a JSON object, where the keys are the names of the event parameters, and the values are the values of the event parameters. You can select from this column using the following sample format, `decoded_log:from::string` or more generally, `decoded_log:<event_param>::datatype`. See below for a full sample query. The `full_decoded_logs` column contains the same information, as well as additional fields such as the datatype of the decoded data. You may need to laterally flatten this column to query the data.
      
Sample query for USDC Transfer events:
```sql select  tx_hash, block_number, contract_address, decoded_log:from::string as from_address, decoded_log:to::string as to_address, decoded_log:value::integer as value from ethereum.core.fact_decoded_event_logs where contract_address = lower('0xA0b86991c6218b36c1d19D4a2e9Eb0cE3606eB48') and block_number between 16400000 and 16405000 and event_name = 'Transfer' limit 50```'

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed. This field will not be unique in this table, as a given transaction can include multiple events.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address interacted with for a given event.

TOPIC_0.name: TOPIC_0
TOPIC_0.comment: The first topic of the event, which is a unique identifier for the event.

TOPIC_1.name: TOPIC_1
TOPIC_1.comment: The second topic of the event, if applicable.

TOPIC_2.name: TOPIC_2
TOPIC_2.comment: The third topic of the event, if applicable.

TOPIC_3.name: TOPIC_3
TOPIC_3.comment: The fourth topic of the event, if applicable.

EVENT_REMOVED.name: EVENT_REMOVED
EVENT_REMOVED.comment: Whether the event has been removed from the transaction.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address at the transaction level.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The to address at the transaction level.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of the contract call.

EVENT_NAME.name: EVENT_NAME
EVENT_NAME.comment: The name of the event, as defined in the contract ABI.

FULL_DECODED_LOG.name: FULL_DECODED_LOG
FULL_DECODED_LOG.comment: The full decoded log, including the event name, the event parameters, and the data type of the event parameters.

DECODED_LOG.name: DECODED_LOG
DECODED_LOG.comment: The flattened decoded log, where the keys are the names of the event parameters, and the values are the values of the event parameters.

CONTRACT_NAME.name: CONTRACT_NAME
CONTRACT_NAME.comment: The name of the contract, if the contract has a name() function.

metadata.name: FACT_EVENT_LOGS
metadata.comment: This table contains flattened event logs from transactions on this EVM blockchain. Transactions may have multiple events, which are denoted by the event index for a transaction hash. Therefore, this table is unique on the combination of transaction hash and event index. Please see `fact_decoded_event_logs` or `ez_decoded_event_logs` for the decoded event logs.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed. This field will not be unique in this table, as a given transaction can include multiple events.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address interacted with for a given event.

TOPIC_0.name: TOPIC_0
TOPIC_0.comment: The first topic of the event, which is a unique identifier for the event.

TOPIC_1.name: TOPIC_1
TOPIC_1.comment: The second topic of the event, if applicable.

TOPIC_2.name: TOPIC_2
TOPIC_2.comment: The third topic of the event, if applicable.

TOPIC_3.name: TOPIC_3
TOPIC_3.comment: The fourth topic of the event, if applicable.

EVENT_REMOVED.name: EVENT_REMOVED
EVENT_REMOVED.comment: Whether the event has been removed from the transaction.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address at the transaction level.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The to address at the transaction level.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of this transaction.

TX_SUCCEEDED.name: TX_SUCCEEDED
TX_SUCCEEDED.comment: Whether the transaction was successful, returned as a boolean.

FACT_EVENT_LOGS_ID.name: FACT_EVENT_LOGS_ID
FACT_EVENT_LOGS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was last modified.

TX_STATUS.name: TX_STATUS
TX_STATUS.comment: This column is being deprecated for standardization purposes. Please update your queries to use the `tx_succeeded` column instead by March 10, 2025.

_LOG_ID.name: _LOG_ID
_LOG_ID.comment: This column is being removed for standardization purposes. Please remove or update your queries with `CONCAT(tx_hash, '_', event_index)` to reconstruct `_log_id` by March 10, 2025.

has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

PRIOR_YEAR_ISO_WEEK_START_DATE.comment: ISO date representing the start of the week for PRIOR_YEAR_DATE_DAY

PRIOR_YEAR_WEEK_OF_YEAR.name: PRIOR_YEAR_WEEK_OF_YEAR
PRIOR_YEAR_WEEK_OF_YEAR.comment: number representing the week of the year for PRIOR_YEAR_DATE_DAY

PRIOR_YEAR_ISO_WEEK_OF_YEAR.comment: number representing the ISO week of the year for PRIOR_YEAR_DATE_DAY

PRIOR_YEAR_MONTH_START_DATE.comment: first date of month for PRIOR_YEAR_DATE_DAY

PRIOR_YEAR_MONTH_END_DATE.comment: last date of month for PRIOR_YEAR_DATE_DAY

QUARTER_START_DATE.comment: first date of quarter for DATE_DAY

metadata.name: DIM_EVM_EVENT_ABIS
metadata.comment: This table contains the individual event abis for each all contracts that we have ABIs for. Proxies are rolled up to their implementation.

BLOCKCHAIN.name: BLOCKCHAIN
BLOCKCHAIN.comment: The blockchain that the event abi is for.

PARENT_CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: PARENT_CONTRACT_ADDRESS
PARENT_CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the contract that the event abi is for.

SIMPLE_EVENT_NAME.name: SIMPLE_EVENT_NAME
SIMPLE_EVENT_NAME.comment: The name of the event along with its parameters.

EVENT_SIGNATURE.name: EVENT_SIGNATURE
EVENT_SIGNATURE.comment: The keccak256 signature of the simple event name. This can be used to filter for events in the logs table on topics[0].

START_BLOCK.name: START_BLOCK
START_BLOCK.comment: The block number of the creation of the contract containing the event. This could be from a proxy or an implementation.

END_BLOCK.name: END_BLOCK
END_BLOCK.comment: The block number before a similar event was created in the contract. This could be from a proxy or an implementation.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

DIM_EVM_EVENT_ABIS_ID.name: DIM_EVM_EVENT_ABIS_ID
DIM_EVM_EVENT_ABIS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.
